text.title: Stakeholder Register Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the stakeholder-register methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Identify groups as groups (e.g. 'Security Council', 'Beta Customers') when a single person is not authoritative.
rule.rationale: Splitting a group into individuals loses the fact that authority is collective.

rule.statement: Use evidence (org chart, budget authority, RACI) for Power rating — not gut feel.
rule.rationale: Two PMs produce different ratings without anchored evidence; anchoring to org-chart and budget makes ratings defensible.

rule.statement: Verify attitude through direct conversation, not from written communication inference.
rule.rationale: Written communication is systematically polite; agents reading transcripts over-classify as 'Supportive'.

rule.statement: Store attitude as 'unknown' when no evidence exists in inputs — never invent an attitude.
rule.rationale: An agent that infers 'supportive' from polite tone produces a false register.

rule.statement: Keep PII (email, phone, salary) in a separate access-controlled file; the in-repo register holds only ID and role.

rationale.source: Privacy practice (GDPR Art. 9 if attitude implies political opinion)

rule.rationale: A register committed to a shared repo exposes sensitive contact and compensation data.

rule.statement: Refresh the register after each stage gate; archive the previous version.
rule.rationale: A stakeholder who was Low-Low at kickoff can become High-High at launch; archived history shows when and why.

list.item: Fully internal personal-tool project with only you and your manager
Pure agile team building for itself with one PO and no external dependencies
Pre-discovery exploration where stakeholders are not yet defined
